feedback.text: Thank you for the opportunity to bring my granite of sand into this fight. I would like to start by asking you to help our community fight in favour of Stop Killing Games. Also, with the new controversies that are emerging, I would like to ask that you help our community to fight against: 1.- stop paying processors’ monopoly so that they cannot control the industry, preventing companies from being able to earn money. 2.- stop censorship of online content, provided that it is legal and does not infringe the laws laid down. 3.- stop the attempt to control and monitor persons and the content to which they are free to access. I have been reporting fairly on all the controversies that are happening, and I would like to see in the near future and in the distant future we have the possibility to keep the products in which I invested money in a minimally functional state. With regard to the other controversies, it seems to me to be a major problem not only of privacy but also of security for individuals and their data online. By forcing us to enter our data and verify our identity, we are entrusting highly exploitable information to bodies that do not know or do not know whether they are reliable, either as natural persons or as online identities. For me, in particular, video games are everything: a source of happiness, entertainment and great help in socialising. I would not like, by a law that seeks to protect minors, I, as an adult, lose the ability to access and use these video games. I understand the need to seek to protect the child, but definitively the way in which this law is being applied is detrimental to both the security and privacy of individuals. That is why I would like to take into account my view as a European citizen, in order to prevent these policies from spreading, and that the Stop Killing Games movement can go ahead, offering us the possibility to maintain these gambling services, entertainment and access to information without the need to enter highly exploitable and personal data online. Thank you very much for reading. I hope that my opinion will serve for the reader of this message.
